reports.text:
ICICI Securities write up:

*Q1FY27 Banking provisional update – Aggregate figures ~65% of the market*

Aggregate of 65% of market has reported provisional business update. 

Loan growth has been 2.6% QoQ and 16% YoY. The loan growth is strong given the seasonality though YoY figures is on the lower side (vs RBI data). We would believe more in bottom-up figure as RBI has date inconsistency.

It looks like reported systemic growth would move-up further on next fortnightly update. 

Privates have an upper hand on QoQ at 3.3%, led by large private while PSB have done 2% QoQ. On YoY basis, PSBs are still ahead at 16.4% YoY vs 15.8% for private.
 
On Deposits side, aggregate growth is 1.4% QoQ and 12% YoY.

Privates have done 2% QoQ and 14% YoY while PSBs have done 1% QoQ and 10.6% YoY.
Uptick in LCR at PSBs could still help PSBs in the near term.

CG POWER :  Prime Minister Narendra Modi visited the CG Semi OSAT facility in Sanand, Gujarat, ahead of its inauguration

The facility, developed with an investment of over ₹7,500 cr, is among the first four projects approved under the India Semiconductor Mission (ISM)
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
CG POWER: STARTS COMMERCIAL PRODUCTION AT SEMICONDUCTOR OSAT FACILITY 🏭🔬

• 🚀 CG Semi has commenced commercial production at its G1 OSAT (Semiconductor Assembly & Test) facility in Sanand, Gujarat

• 📦 G1 facility has a peak capacity of up to 300 million units per year

• 💰 CG Semi is investing over ₹7,600 crore over five years to develop G1 and G2 OSAT facilities

• 🏗️ While G1 has started production, G2 is under development and will significantly expand manufacturing capacity

• 🇮🇳 The facility is a JV of CG Power, Renesas Electronics and Stars Microelectronics, supporting India's semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem

🟢 Impact: Positive (Marks a key milestone in CG Power's semiconductor business and strengthens its long-term growth opportunity in India's chip manufacturing ecosystem.)

reports.text:
LODHA: CO TO STOP QUARTERLY PRE-SALES PRE-RELEASES 🔴🔴

• 📊 Going forward, Lodha will report audited Revenue and PAT as its primary business performance metrics

• 📅 Revenue, PAT and key operating indicators, including pre-sales, will be disclosed along with quarterly results

• 📝 The company will no longer issue separate quarterly operating performance/pre-sales pre-releases

• ✅ Management said the performance for the quarter ended was in line with its guidance

🟡 Impact: Neutral (This is a change in disclosure and reporting practice, with no change to business performance or guidance.)

BDL ; BRAHMOS EXPORTS MAY EXPAND TO INDONESIA ; ET REPORT

• 🇮🇩 India is in talks to supply BrahMos supersonic cruise missiles to Indonesia

• 🌏 If finalized, Indonesia would join the Philippines and Vietnam in operating BrahMos systems

• ⚓ The deal could strengthen coastal defence capabilities across Southeast Asia and enhance regional deterrence

• 🤝 The potential export further supports India's push to expand defence exports and strategic partnerships in the Indo-Pacific

🟢 Impact: Positive (Favourable for India's defence export ecosystem, with potential benefits for companies involved in the BrahMos supply chain and missile manufacturing.)

.HDFCBank - 1Q27 Biz Update: Better but not the best as one wanted. 

• Gross loans stood at Rs30.6 trn, up 15% YoY and 3% QoQ – highest YoY growth since merger. AUM was Rs31.3 trn, up 12% YoY and 2% QoQ.

• Deposits came in at Rs31.7 trn, up 15% YoY and 2% QoQ. **On average basis seasonal sequential pace is maintained but slower YoY, barring CASA which has base effect.**

• CASA stood at Rs10.3 trn, up 9% YoY but down 3% QoQ (from CA decline generally). **CASA ratio was at 32.3% **- This is now almost like south based regional pvt banks and with FCNR this could look more lower.

• Term deposits grew strong by 17% YoY and 5% QoQ.

• C-D ratio (on gross loans) was 96.5%, compared to 95.3% in 4Q26 & 96.0% in 1Q26.

🏢 Tridev Infraestates – NCLT Approves Share Capital Reduction

• The NCLT has approved the reduction of the company's share capital.

• The paid-up value of each equity share will be reduced from ₹10 to ₹5, representing a 50% capital reduction.

• Thereafter, every 2 equity shares of ₹5 each will be consolidated into 1 equity share of ₹10.

• As a result, the issued and paid-up equity share capital will reduce from ₹6.53 crore (65,25,400 shares) to ₹3.26 crore (32,62,700 shares).

Q1 FY2027 earnings season is about to begin. 📊

The next 40–50 days will be crucial for portfolio allocation.

Go aggressive on companies delivering strong numbers.

Reduce exposure to businesses where the story is weakening.

This is where next year’s winners and losers start getting separated.

Companies that are changing business mix from lower margin to higher , legacy to future , commodity to specialty , broad to niche     

This is where I am seeing profit pools emerging as execution happens in many of them , and these are repeatable patterns as a broader mix of the value chain is captured     

Typically this transition takes time , and gives opportunities for the prepared , but is very lucrative , but this will only be available for those who were prepared  

Last year when sansera transition into ADS was happening many did not understand , but this year when stock is up 2x , there is so much chatter about sansera becoming more into aerospace , price changes they way people look at business mix delta , change in business mix has very high hit rates if more value is captured and management is good

WEEKLY INSIGHTS | Pharma, IT & Investing Lessons

1️⃣ Gufic Biosciences – Execution Phase Begins

Key Highlights
- Indore capex completed
- Commercialisation phase begins
- Complex injectables focus
- Strong innovation-led platform

Why It Stands Out
- End-to-end capabilities in lyophilized injectables, liquids, ampoules, microspheres and NDDS under one roof.
- Transitioning from a distributor-led export model to an IP-owned, marketing authorisation-driven global strategy.
- Large manufacturing scale (100K+ vial batches) offers meaningful cost advantages for exports.
- No major greenfield capex planned over the next two years, allowing focus to shift towards utilisation, execution and profitability.
- International approvals continue to expand across Europe, South Africa and other regulated markets.
- Validation work underway for a GLP-1 lyophilised vial CDMO programme.
- Management expects operating leverage as plant utilisation improves over the coming years.

Key Takeaway
- Gufic appears to be moving from an investment cycle into an execution cycle, where future value creation will depend on capacity utilisation, global product launches and successful commercial execution rather than additional capital expenditure.

---

2️⃣ Coforge – AI as a Growth Enabler

Key Highlights
- Strong Investor Day presentation
- AI viewed as a growth driver
- Clear long-term strategy
- Strong management confidence

Why It Stands Out
- Management views AI as an opportunity to expand services rather than compress revenues.
- Focus remains on integrating AI across delivery, client solutions and internal operations.
- Detailed business-unit presentations provide strong insights into future growth areas and industry trends.
- Leadership continues to communicate with high clarity on execution priorities.

Key Takeaway
- Coforge's messaging suggests that AI can become a demand accelerator for high-quality IT services firms, with execution and client adoption likely to determine long-term winners.

---

3️⃣ Investing Lesson – Research Alone Isn't Enough

Key Highlights
- Research is only one part
- Execution drives returns
- Position sizing matters
- Conviction creates wealth

Key Takeaway
- Successful investing depends not only on finding quality businesses, but also on buying them at sensible valuations, sizing positions appropriately, having conviction through market cycles, and remaining patient. Research identifies opportunities, while disciplined execution determines long-term outcomes.

Disclaimer: This post is only for educational and learning purposes. It reflects personal observations and should not be construed as investment advice or a Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.

GOVT MAY REVAMP GOLD MONETISATION SCHEME; JEWELLERS LIKELY TO BE ADDED AS COLLECTION PARTNERS

The Central Government is expected to announce a revamped Gold Monetisation Scheme (GMS) within the next two weeks, according to sources.

Under the proposed framework, jewellers may be allowed to act as Collection Partners, enabling them to collect and aggregate household gold deposits before routing them into the formal GMS ecosystem—a role previously limited to banks.

The move aims to mobilise a larger share of India's estimated 30,000 tonnes of idle household gold, reduce dependence on bullion imports, and improve participation in a scheme that has seen limited success since its launch

reports.text:
BSE volumes down 37% Against the first week of June'26

Post-RBI circular volume check (effective 1-Jul'26): 1st two trading sessions on BSE

We reviewed BSE's initial volume trends following the RBI circular that took effect on 1st July 2026.

# 0DTE (expiry day) — 2nd July'26: Premium turnover came in at Rs 547.02bn, ~19% below the 1QFY27 average expiry-day turnover (Rs 679.18bn) and about 11% below the June'26 average (Rs 617.29bn). Against the 1st week of June'26 (Rs 716.80bn), it is ~24% lower.

# 1DTE (one day to expiry) — 1st July'26: Turnover stood at Rs 355.11bn, ~9% below the 1QFY27 average (Rs 389.49bn) and about 11% below the June'26 average (Rs 397.25bn). Against the first week of June'26 (Rs 561.28bn), it is ~37% lower.

While it is early trends, however, Jul'26 volumes have started on a much lower number as compared to Jun'26, thus posing risks for downward revision to estimates in premium ADTO.

OVOBEL FOODS: CRISIL DOWNGRADES CREDIT RATING TO 'BB/STABLE' ⚠️📉

• ⭐ CRISIL Ratings revised the company's long-term bank loan rating to CRISIL BB/Stable from CRISIL BB+/Stable, while reaffirming the short-term rating at CRISIL A4+ under the 'Issuer Not Cooperating' category

• 🏦 The revised rating applies to ₹29.90 crore of bank loan facilities, including cash credit and term loans

• ⚠️ CRISIL cited non-availability of adequate information from the management for continuous monitoring, leading to the 'Issuer Not Cooperating' classification

• 📝 Ovobel Foods said it is initiating the withdrawal process and believes the rating agency did not adequately consider its current financial performance, which could impact borrowing costs

• 📢 The company said it will keep the stock exchanges informed of any further material developments regarding the rating matter 

🔴 Impact: Negative (The credit rating downgrade and 'Issuer Not Cooperating' classification could increase borrowing costs and weigh on lender and investor confidence.)

TATA POWER: COMMISSIONS 100.8 MW JEWALI WIND PROJECT IN MAHARASHTRA 🌬️⚡

• 🌬️ Tata Power Renewable Energy Ltd. (TPREL) has commissioned its 100.8 MW Jewali Wind Project in Dharashiv, Maharashtra

• ⚡ The project is expected to generate 299 million units of clean electricity annually and offset over 245 million kg of CO₂ emissions every year

• 🏙️ The power generated will be supplied to Tata Power Mumbai Distribution, supporting its Renewable Purchase Obligation (RPO) compliance

• 📈 With this commissioning, TPREL's total renewable utility capacity has reached 11.6 GW, including 6.7 GW operational and 4.9 GW under implementation

• 🎯 The project strengthens Tata Power's renewable portfolio and supports its goal of achieving 100% clean energy generation by 2045 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The commissioning expands Tata Power's renewable capacity, enhances clean energy generation and strengthens long-term growth visibility.)

reports.text:
Powerica
Recently, there is lot of hype around anything related to data centers...

This is a newly listed stock, and there was certainly lot of hoopla around it as 15% of the orderbook is from data centers and fast growing.

Did some deep dive - 
It seems the company is more of a systems integrator + EPC business for gensets. It gets critical parts like engine, alternator etc. from Cummins and builds the gensets along with installation.

Also, all the major genset orders from hyperscalers go directly to Cummins. Only the small-scale desi data center or edge data center orders come to Powerica

Basically, its a low-tech low-margin business...nothing to get excited about.

The other business is wind power - IPP + EPC, which is having its own challenges.

Power Equipment Sector | Government Grants 2-Year PPO Exemption to Four Chinese Vendors

Key Highlights
- 2-year PPO exemption
- Four Chinese vendors
- PSU tender eligibility
- Transformer sector impact

Key Developments
- TBEA: EHV transformers & reactors
- Nanjing: GIS & HVDC insulators
- Northeast: 400/765 kV GIS
- Taikai: 400/765 kV GIS

Key Takeaway
- The government's two-year exemption from Public Procurement Order (PPO) restrictions allows four Chinese electrical equipment manufacturers to participate in PSU tenders for high-voltage transformers and GIS switchgear until domestic manufacturing capacity expands. The move could increase competitive intensity and moderate order opportunities for domestic players such as GE Vernova, CG Power, Siemens India and Hitachi Energy India, particularly in the high-voltage transmission equipment segment.

Disclaimer: This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.

Power Equipment Stocks in Focus – Chinese Supplier Exemptions Raise Competitive Concerns

The market is reacting to reports that the Ministry of Finance has granted exemptions to four Chinese-linked entities to supply equipment for critical power projects, following a request from the Ministry of Power.

The exempted entities reportedly include:

TBEA Energy India
Nanjing Electric India
New North East Electric India
Taikai Electric India

Potential Market Impact:

Negative: Bharat Heavy Electricals Limited, CG Power and Industrial Solutions and Hitachi Energy India could face sentiment pressure if Chinese suppliers are allowed to participate in select high-value transmission and power equipment tenders.

Tata Steel expects €500 million EBITDA from its Dutch operations in FY27!
Chairman N Chandrasekaran said the Netherlands business is on track to deliver strong earnings this year, with a long-term goal of €800 million to €1 billion. Despite challenges like environmental rules in Europe, the company is confident about future growth.

Global Semiconductor Supply Chain Remains Highly Concentrated

Key Highlights:

🏭 TSMC controls over 80% of the world's leading-edge (<16nm) chip production, powering most AI servers and premium smartphones.

🌏 Taiwan, South Korea, and China account for ~85% of global semiconductor foundry revenue, highlighting regional concentration.

💾 Samsung and SK Hynix produce ~70% of the world's DRAM memory chips.

⚙️ ASML holds a near-100% monopoly in EUV lithography, the critical technology required to manufacture chips below 7nm.

🇺🇸 US companies generate around 60% of global chip design revenue, while China accounts for ~38% of global OSAT (chip packaging and testing) capacity.

🏗️ 56% of global wafer capacity still lies in mature nodes (≥34nm), creating an opportunity for emerging semiconductor manufacturing hubs like India.

reports.text:
Bernstein on Trent

O-P, TP cut to Rs 3500 from Rs 5000

While Zudio has overshadowed Westside in store adds over last 5+ years, Westside is still 40% of Trent’s total retail area & surprised on store

additions in F26 after lackluster F23-F25 years

Proprietary store data indicates 3 things for Westside

(1) Store rationalization is over and helped Trent move away from certain cities & markets,

(2) Stores additions indicate focus in Top-100 cities only with city-densification underway

(3) Pin-densification is not visible (unlike what Trent did with Zudio)

Believe approx +50 stores/year is an achievable target for next 4-5 years, which will take them to ~500-550 stores.

For context, Pantaloons has 400 may add 20/year.

Hence, do not see whitespace as an issue and with a clearer strategy, expect them to achieve this target.

CLSA on NHPC

High Conviction O-P, TP Rs 117

NDR takeaways,

Key message from NHPC was FY26 saw start of two of its largest projects, driving regulated equity (RegEq) up 25% YoY, but a delay in Parbati 2 (P2) tariff & restoration of Teesta deflated PAT by c.20%.

Management addressed clients’ biggest concerns about PAT growth by clarifying its conservative accounting policy has deflated FY26 PAT, but once it secures final tariff for P2 HEP, full PAT will be recognised retrospectively with interest, so there is no loss of value on an NPV basis.

NHPC is biggest beneficiary of Indus water treaty being put in abeyance and it has awarded two of the four hydro projects already, which are sub four-year projects

Forecast REE (ex-minority) growth of 90% over FY26-30CL versus 31% growth over past five years

With a step-change in REE, forecast its EPS to double & its ROE to expand 750bps over FY26-28CL.

Atharva Polyplast looks more than just a plastic component maker.
What stands out is its end-to-end manufacturing setup — from design and tooling to moulding, finishing, and assembly — all under one roof.

A few things make the story interesting:

Strong compliance stack helps it qualify for global customers
Margins have improved sharply, driven by a better product mix
Engineering services can become a meaningful third growth engine
The company has scale-up headroom, backed by land, capacity, and premium market opportunity

In simple words, Atharva seems to be positioning itself as a precision manufacturing partner for global players, not just a commodity supplier.

If execution stays on track, this could be a business worth tracking closely.

**Prestige Estates – Morgan Stanley View**

Morgan Stanley remains bullish on Prestige Estates, citing strong pre-sales momentum, resilient demand, growing leasing income and attractive embedded value in its rental portfolio.

**Brokerage View**
- Rating: Overweight
- Target price: ₹1,920
- Positive long-term outlook
- Strong execution continues

**Key Takeaways**
- Strong start to 1QFY27
- Pre-sales: ₹6,300 crore in first two months
- Achieved ~18% of FY27 estimate
- Ahead of expected run-rate

**Demand Outlook**
- Demand remains healthy
- Stable footfalls and conversions
- ASPs remain resilient
- No meaningful AI-related demand impact in Bengaluru or Hyderabad

**Leasing Update**
- Tower X, BKC: ~70% leased
- Mahalakshmi project: ~10% leased
- Leasing rate around ₹400/sq. ft.
- Commercial portfolio gaining traction

**Hospitality Business**
- Hospitality listing may be deferred
- Awaiting completion of Aerocity hotel
- Aerocity project to have 778 keys
- Listing could unlock value later

**Rental Potential**
- FY29 rental income potential: ₹4,000 crore
- Assumed cap rate: 7.5%
- Rental portfolio worth nearly two-thirds of Enterprise Value
- Highlights undervalued residential and hospitality businesses

**Why It Matters**
- Strong pre-sales momentum
- Healthy commercial leasing
- Significant rental income growth
- Attractive valuation support

**Key Takeaway**
- Morgan Stanley believes **Prestige Estates is well-positioned for sustained growth, supported by strong residential demand, expanding commercial leasing, significant rental income potential and hidden value in its hospitality and residential businesses, while maintaining an Overweight rating with a target price of ₹1,920.**

__Disclaimer: I am not a SEBI-registered Analyst. This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

**Godrej Properties – Morgan Stanley View**

Morgan Stanley remains bullish on Godrej Properties, highlighting strong pre-sales momentum, improving profitability and a long runway for growth.

**Brokerage View**
- Rating: Overweight
- Target price: ₹2,100
- Positive long-term outlook
- Strong execution continues

**Key Takeaways**
- Strong start to 1QFY27
- Pre-sales: ₹7,100 crore in first two months

**Profitability Outlook**
- ROE target: 20% by FY28

**Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs) – CLSA View**

CLSA believes the recent sharp fall in crude oil prices provides near-term earnings relief for India's oil marketing companies, but cautions against extrapolating these gains over the long term.

**Sector View**
- Positive near-term outlook
- Lower crude boosts marketing margins
- Earnings recovery expected
- Long-term risks remain

**Key Positives**
- Sharp decline in crude prices
- Retail fuel prices likely to remain unchanged
- Higher auto-fuel marketing margins
- Opportunity to recover past losses

**Why Margins Improve**
- OMCs incurred losses when crude surged
- Stable pump prices allow margin recovery
- Similar situation seen in 2022
- Near-term profitability improves

**Key Risk**
- Government may reverse ₹10/litre excise duty cut
- Fiscal deficit targets could drive policy change
- Margin normalization possible
- Relief may be temporary

**Valuation View**
- Current valuations imply ~₹3/litre marketing margin
- Sustainable only if Brent remains around US$70–75/bbl
- Higher crude could pressure profitability
- Limited upside if oil prices rise

**Preferred Outlook**
- Near-term earnings supportive
- Crude below US$70/bbl is ideal
- Policy decisions remain key
- Monitor fuel taxation closely

**Why It Matters**
- Lower crude boosts profitability
- Excise policy is a major variable
- Margin sustainability uncertain
- Oil prices remain key driver

**Key Takeaway**
- CLSA believes **the recent fall in crude prices offers a welcome earnings recovery for IOC, BPCL and HPCL through stronger fuel marketing margins. However, investors should remain cautious, as any reversal of the ₹10/litre excise duty cut or a rebound in Brent crude above US$70–75/bbl could significantly reduce the sector's long-term upside.**

__Disclaimer: I am not a SEBI-registered Analyst. This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

**Dr. Reddy's Laboratories – Jefferies View**

Jefferies remains cautious on Dr. Reddy's Laboratories despite raising earnings estimates under an optimistic scenario, citing an unfavorable risk-reward profile.

**Brokerage View**
- Rating: Underperform
- Target price: ₹1,040
- Risk-reward remains unfavorable
- Limited upside at current valuations

**Key Growth Drivers**
- Semaglutide in Canada
- Abatacept biosimilar in the US
- Higher market share expected in Canada
- US launch expected in early CY27

**Key Assumptions**
- Semaglutide gains share despite pricing pressure
- Abatacept launch proceeds despite pending plant observations
- Optimistic execution assumptions
- Strong contribution from key products

**Financial Outlook**
- FY27–FY28 EPS estimates raised by 3–9%
- Optimistic fair value: ₹1,540
- Fair value based on 24x Mar-28 EPS
- Implies only ~13% upside in bullish scenario

**Jefferies View**
- Upside appears limited
- Valuation already reflects optimism
- Execution risks remain
- Maintains cautious stance

**Why It Matters**
- Key launches support earnings
- Regulatory risks persist
- Limited valuation upside
- Unfavorable risk-reward

**Key Takeaway**
- Jefferies believes **Dr. Reddy's key growth drivers—Semaglutide in Canada and Abatacept in the US—could improve earnings, but even under optimistic assumptions the upside appears modest. With regulatory and execution risks still present, it maintains an Underperform rating with a target price of ₹1,040.**

**Nomura on Delhi EV Policy & City Gas Companies**

Nomura believes Delhi's EV Policy 2026–30 marks a structural shift towards mandatory EV adoption.

**Policy Highlights**
- ₹150 billion investment
- Effective July 1, 2026
- EV registration mandates
- Charging infrastructure push

**Impact on IGL**
- Buy maintained
- Negative for auto-CNG growth
- CNG car segment at risk
- Existing fleet limits near-term impact

**Impact on Peers**
- MGL: No immediate impact
- Future risk if Maharashtra follows
- Gujarat Gas least impacted
- PNG business remains resilient

**Key Takeaway**
- Nomura believes **Delhi's EV policy is structurally negative for IGL's long-term auto-CNG volume growth**, while **MGL faces no immediate impact and Gujarat Gas remains relatively insulated** due to its industrial-heavy gas portfolio.

*Not SEBI registered. For educational purposes only.*

**Citi on Delhi EV Policy**

Citi believes Delhi's EV policy is supportive for EV adoption but sees execution challenges.

**Brokerage View**
- Positive for EVs
- ICE registrations phased out
- Hybrid clarity awaited
- Execution challenges remain

**Policy Highlights**
- Only e3Ws from Jan 2027
- Only e2Ws from Apr 2028
- ₹50,000 subsidy for e3Ws
- ₹30,000 subsidy for e2Ws

**Key Concerns**
- Limited charging network
- Few e-motorcycle options
- Border enforcement challenges
- Hybrid policy unclear

**Market Impact**
- Delhi contributes 5% of PV sales
- 3% of 2W sales
- 2% of 3W sales
- <3% of IGL volumes

**Key Takeaway**
- Citi views the policy as **supportive for EV adoption**, but believes **charging infrastructure, product availability and implementation challenges** could moderate its near-term impact.

*Not SEBI registered. For educational purposes only.*
